vectors.description: Empty capability set MUST yield bytes32(0). Marks a trivial-public payment (per receipt.md §6).
Single capability (hides:amount:v1). Computes keccak256(abi.encode(bytes32[1])) over the single id.

input._note: When _inline_capability_ids is present, the runner computes each id via the capabilityId derivation and uses the inline order as the wire order. This keeps fixtures readable without parallel placeholder arrays.

vectors.description: Two-cap set in inline-declaration order: [hides:sender, hides:amount]. The runner canonicalizes (dedupe + bytes32 ascending sort) before hashing, so the wire order shown here is descriptive only.
spec_refs: HSP.md §3.1.3 'sort lexicographically as bytes32 (ascending byte order)'

vectors.description: Same two caps as 'two-caps-canonical-order' but listed in reversed inline order: [hides:amount, hides:sender]. The recomputed hash MUST equal the previous vector's hash, proving canonicalization is verifier-side and wire order is irrelevant.
spec_refs: HSP.md §5.1 step 3 'Wire array order and duplicates are tolerated by construction'

vectors.description: Three entries with one cap appearing twice on the wire: [hides:sender, hides:amount, hides:sender]. Canonicalization MUST deduplicate, yielding the same hash as the no-dup case.
spec_refs: HSP.md §3.1.3 step 2 'Deduplicate (keep one copy of each bytes32 value)'

vectors.description: Realistic mandate: hides:amount:v1 + attests:kyc:v1[level=full] + attests:sanctions:v1. Exercises mixed-namespace canonicalization across three different cap shapes (param-less hides, param-less attests, parameterized attests).
